sources.note: National comparisons are presented in constant dollars to reflect real changes over time

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.description:
Where possible, comparisons are made between Canadians at the same age across time rather than between generations. This approach helps isolate structural economic changes from normal life-cycle effects, since wealth and homeownership tend to rise with age.

All long-run comparisons are inflation-adjusted to constant dollars. Calculations such as hours of work required to pay tuition are author calculations based on publicly reported figures.

limitations.title: Limitations
limitations.description: National averages may mask significant regional variation, particularly in housing markets such as Toronto and Vancouver. Changes in survey methods over time may also affect comparability across years. Generational labels are used as a narrative tool, but underlying analysis is based on age cohorts.
